tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read document content (DOCX, ODT, or Google Doc). Output is token-limited (~14k tokens) by default with pagination metadata (has_more, next_offset). Use offset/limit to paginate.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

google_doc_id.description: Google Doc ID or URL (alternative to file_path). Extract from URL: docs.google.com/document/d/{ID}/edit

offset.description: 1-based paragraph offset for pagination. Negative values count from end.

limit.description: Max paragraphs to return. When omitted, output is token-limited to ~14k tokens with pagination.

comment_rendering.description: How to render comments in read_file output. Use "paragraph_notes" (default) for paragraph-local comment threads, "inline_markers" to add `[cm-start:N]`/`[cm-end:N]` milestones in TOON output (combined with the thread blocks), "endnotes" to collect threaded comments into a trailing #COMMENTS block in TOON output, or "none" for the legacy output with no comment rendering.

show_formatting.description: When true (default), shows inline formatting tags (<b>, <i>, <u>, <highlighting>, <a>). When false, emits plain text with no inline tags.

include_fingerprint.description: When true and format="json", include a portable content_fingerprint ("sha256:nfkc:<32hex>") on each paragraph. Read-only metadata derived from the paragraph's normalized visible text; NOT an edit anchor. Edit tools accept only `_bk_*` IDs. No effect on TOON/simple output. Ignored for Google Docs and ODT.

include_footnotes.description: When true and format="json", attach a `footnotes` array ({id, display_number, text}) to each paragraph node for the footnotes anchored to it. Windowed to the returned slice (a paginated walk returns each footnote exactly once) and counted toward the read token budget. Footnotes with an empty body or no anchored paragraph are excluded — use get_footnotes for the authoritative full enumeration. No effect on TOON/simple output. Ignored for Google Docs and ODT. Default: false.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search paragraphs with regex. Use file_path for session-based search, file_paths for stateless multi-file search, or google_doc_id for Google Docs. ODT supported via file_path (single-file) only.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

google_doc_id.description: Google Doc ID or URL (alternative to file_path). Extract from URL: docs.google.com/document/d/{ID}/edit

file_paths.description: Multiple file paths for stateless multi-file search. No session created.

search_xml.description: When true, search raw XML (word/document.xml) instead of paragraph text.

include_context.description: When false, skip document view context (list labels, headers) for faster results. Default: true.

tools.name: batch_edit
tools.description: Single-agent front door for applying multiple edit steps (replace_text, insert_paragraph) to a document in one call. Validates all steps first, rejects conflicts before applying anything, then executes valid steps sequentially. Accepts inline steps or a plan_file_path JSON array.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

steps.description: JSON array of edit steps. Each step needs step_id, operation, and operation-specific fields.

plan_file_path.description: Path to a .json file containing an array of edit steps. Mutually exclusive with steps.

tools.name: replace_text
tools.description: Replace text in a paragraph by provider paragraph id, preserving formatting where supported. Supports DOCX, ODT, and Google Docs.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

google_doc_id.description: Google Doc ID or URL (alternative to file_path). Extract from URL: docs.google.com/document/d/{ID}/edit

normalize_first.description: Merge format-identical adjacent runs before searching. Useful when text is fragmented across runs.

tools.name: insert_paragraph
tools.description: Insert a paragraph before/after an anchor paragraph by paragraph id. Supports DOCX, ODT, and Google Docs. (ODT paragraph ids are positional and shift after insertion — re-read before further edits.)

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

google_doc_id.description: Google Doc ID or URL (alternative to file_path). Extract from URL: docs.google.com/document/d/{ID}/edit

style_source_id.description: Paragraph _bk_* ID to clone formatting (pPr and template run) from instead of the positional anchor. Falls back to anchor with a warning if not found.

tools.name: save
tools.description: Save document. For DOCX: saves clean and/or tracked changes output. For ODT: saves an .odt package. For Google Docs: checkpoint (default) returns revisionId, or snapshot exports as DOCX.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

google_doc_id.description: Google Doc ID or URL (alternative to file_path). Extract from URL: docs.google.com/document/d/{ID}/edit

fail_on_rebuild_fallback.description: When true, return an error instead of a destructive output if the comparison engine falls back to rebuild mode (which destroys table structure). Default: false.

tools.name: export
tools.description: Export a document to a portable rendering (Markdown, semantic HTML, or plain text). Writes an output file (default: source path with the format extension, e.g. .md, .html, or .txt) and returns its path, byte count, and the rendered content (under `content`). Intentionally lossy (no round-trip); HTML is the semantic tier, not pixel-faithful. DOCX only — Google Docs is not supported.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

output_path.description: Where to write the rendering. Defaults to the source path with the format extension.

allow_overwrite.description: Overwrite output_path if it already exists. Default: false.

include_markdown.description: Include the rendered content (under `content`) in the response. Default: true; set false for large documents.

tools.name: convert_to_odt
tools.description: Convert a DOCX document to OpenDocument Text (.odt) using the native model-to-model converter (no LibreOffice involved). Writes the .odt (default: source path with the .odt extension), validates ODF packaging safety before writing, and returns the output path plus a `lossiness` summary itemizing every downgraded construct. Conversion is semantic and intentionally lossy: text, headings, bold/italic/underline, hyperlinks, lists, and tables are mapped; richer styling, tracked changes, comments, and headers/footers are not. DOCX in, ODT out — Google Docs and .odt inputs are not supported.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

output_path.description: Where to write the .odt. Defaults to the source path with the .odt extension.

allow_overwrite.description: Overwrite output_path if it already exists. Default: false.

tools.name: format_layout
tools.description: Apply layout controls (paragraph spacing, table row height, cell padding). Google Docs supports paragraph spacing only.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

google_doc_id.description: Google Doc ID or URL (alternative to file_path). Extract from URL: docs.google.com/document/d/{ID}/edit

tools.name: accept_changes
tools.description: Accept all tracked changes in the document body, producing a clean document with no revision markup. Returns acceptance stats.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

tools.name: has_tracked_changes
tools.description: Check whether the document body contains tracked-change markers (insertions, deletions, moves, and property-change records). Read-only.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

tools.name: get_file_status
tools.description: Get file/session metadata including edit count, normalization stats, and cache info. Supports DOCX, ODT, and Google Docs.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

google_doc_id.description: Google Doc ID or URL (alternative to file_path). Extract from URL: docs.google.com/document/d/{ID}/edit

tools.name: close_file
tools.description: Close an open file session, or close all sessions with explicit confirmation. Supports DOCX, ODT, and Google Docs.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

google_doc_id.description: Google Doc ID or URL (alternative to file_path). Extract from URL: docs.google.com/document/d/{ID}/edit

tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Add a comment or threaded reply to a document. Provide target_paragraph_id + anchor_text for root comments, or parent_comment_id for replies. Supports DOCX and ODT (ODT backs comments with office:annotation; threaded replies are DOCX-only).

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

target_paragraph_id.description: Paragraph ID to anchor the comment to (for root comments).

anchor_text.description: Text within the paragraph to anchor the comment to. If omitted, anchors to entire paragraph.

parent_comment_id.description: Parent comment ID for threaded replies.

initials.description: Author initials (defaults to first letter of author name).

tools.name: get_comments
tools.description: Get all comments from the document with IDs, authors, dates, text, and anchored paragraph IDs. Range-anchored DOCX comments also expose optional end_paragraph_id, start_run_index, start_char_offset, end_run_index, and end_char_offset fields describing the covered span. Includes threaded replies (DOCX). Supports DOCX and ODT. Read-only.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

tools.name: delete_comment
tools.description: Delete a comment and all its threaded replies from the document. Cascade-deletes all descendants.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

tools.name: compare_documents
tools.description: Compare two documents and produce a tracked-changes output document. Provide original_file_path + revised_file_path for standalone comparison, or file_path to compare session edits against the original. DOCX and ODF (.odt) support both modes. DOCX stats count insertions/deletions as contiguous ranges, expose atom totals as insertedAtoms/deletedAtoms, and report formatChanges separately from modifiedParagraphs. ODF compares at inline granularity (a modified paragraph is marked up in place — only the changed spans are struck or inserted).

original_file_path.description: Path to the original DOCX or .odt file.

revised_file_path.description: Path to the revised DOCX or .odt file.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

save_to_local_path.description: Path to save the tracked-changes output (DOCX or .odt).

author.description: Author name for track changes. Default: 'Comparison' (DOCX) or the configured AI author (ODF).

tools.name: get_footnotes
tools.description: Get all footnotes from the document with IDs, display numbers, text, and anchored paragraph IDs. Read-only.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

tools.name: add_footnote
tools.description: Add a footnote anchored to a paragraph. Optionally position the reference after specific text using after_text. Note: [^N] markers in read_file output are display-only and not part of the editable text used by replace_text.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

target_paragraph_id.description: Paragraph ID to anchor the footnote to.

after_text.description: Text after which to insert the footnote reference. If omitted, appends at end of paragraph.

tools.name: update_footnote
tools.description: Update the text content of an existing footnote.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

tools.name: delete_footnote
tools.description: Delete a footnote and its reference from the document.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

tools.name: clear_formatting
tools.description: Clear specific run-level formatting (bold, italic, underline, highlight, color, font) from paragraphs.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

paragraph_ids.description: Paragraph IDs to clear formatting from. If omitted, clears from all paragraphs.

tools.name: extract_revisions
tools.description: Extract tracked changes as structured JSON with before/after text per paragraph, revision details, and comments. Supports pagination via offset and limit. Read-only - does not modify the document.

file_path.description: Path to the DOCX or ODT file.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 23 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Server correctly ignores cancellation of unknown request
findings.description: The server remained responsive after receiving notifications/cancelled for a non-existent request.

findings.title: Cancellation race condition timing detected — server responded normally
findings.description: The request/cancel race did not cause a hang; the server responded to one of the two messages.
findings.remediation: No action required — server handled the race gracefully.
